videos.title: I Turned Google Drive Into My Browser's Download Folder | VibeCoding Until I Make $100k | Ep.5
videos.description:
I found a Chrome extension idea on Reddit, built it in 8 hours, and submitted it to the Chrome Web Store, all in one session. In this video I walk you through the full process: finding the idea, architecting the extension, handling Google OAuth in the background script, building the right-click save functionality, and designing the UI using AI tools.

This is Episode 5 of Vibe Coding Until I Make $100k - a series where I build and ship Chrome extensions from scratch and document everything publicly.

What I cover:
- How I found the idea browsing Reddit
- Setting up Google OAuth correctly in a Chrome extension (always in background.js, never the popup)
- Getting a permanent Chrome extension ID for development
- Using Cursor to build the upload functionality
- Designing the UI with ShadeGPT and coding it with Claude
- Creating the logo with AI
- Submitting to the Chrome Web Store

Tech used: Chrome Extensions, Google Drive API, Google OAuth, Cursor, Claude, ChatGPT, JavaScript

Download: still waiting for Chrome webstore's approval!

--
TIMESTAMPS
00:00 - Built a Chrome extension in 8 hours
00:34 - How I found the idea on Reddit
00:56 - Why competing extensions had bad reviews (permissions issue)
01:27 - Setting up Google OAuth via Google Cloud
01:36 - Getting a permanent Chrome extension ID
01:55 - Sending the first prompt to Cursor
02:04 - Key rule: always do auth in background.js, never the popup
02:29 - Building the upload functionality
02:58 - Adding right-click save to Google Drive
03:12 - Designing the UI with ShadeGPT + Claude
03:49 - Creating the logo with ChatGPT
04:14 - Final fixes: Google login, popup, auth verification
04:24 - Publishing to the Chrome Web Store
---

🔔 Subscribe for weekly build-in-public episodes
🌐 codedcitadel.com

videos.title: Export Instagram Conversations in One Click | VibeCoding Until I Make $100k | Ep. 4
videos.description:
No Instagram API key needed, 100% free. 

I built a Chrome extension that exports any Instagram conversation to HTML or TXT in one click - by reverse engineering how Instagram loads messages directly in the browser.

In this episode I walk through the full process: finding a crowded niche and spotting a gap anyway, reverse engineering Instagram's internal API using the network tab, using Claude to write the pagination fetch loop, and my exact workflow for injecting UI that blends natively into Instagram's design.

This is episode 4 of my build-in-public series shipping Chrome extensions on the road to $100k. No sales yet - but that's not the point right now.

Extension live on the Chrome Web Store (link below)
Subscribe to follow the journey


TIMESTAMPS
0:00 - What I built: export any Instagram DM in one click
0:30 - Finding the idea: browsing Instagram Chrome extensions
0:50 - Spotting the gap: only 2 message exporters, both with bad reviews
1:05 - Reverse engineering Instagram's API via the network tab
1:25 - Using Ctrl+F in the network tab to find the exact request
1:40 - How Instagram's pagination works - and how to automate it
1:55 - Sending the API response to Claude to generate the fetch loop
2:10 - Implementing HTML and TXT export formats
2:20 - Extracting Instagram's CSS so the UI blends in natively
2:35 - UX decision: same button in both the popup and the page
2:50 - Setting up the samples folder as AI reference context
3:05 - Finding a unique CSS selector on an obfuscated codebase
3:25 - Writing the Cursor prompt - 30 prompts total, but not complex
3:50 - Fixing the main bug: fetching the wrong conversation on URL change
4:10 - Watching URL changes and reinjecting the script on new users
4:25 - Designing the download icon with Claude + logo with ChatGPT
4:35 - Publishing to the Chrome Web Store
4:45 - No sales yet - and why that's fine at episode 4

#Instagram #ChromeExtension #VibeCoding #BuildInPublic #ReverseEngineering #IndieHacker #SideProject #ChromeExtensionDevelopment #SoloFounder #CodedCitadel

videos.title: I built Claude's missing search feature for free | VibeCoding Until I Make $100k | Ep. 3
videos.description:
Claude's search only lets you search conversation titles - not the actual content inside them. So I built a free Chrome extension to fix it. Total time: 3 hours, including the extension, logo, screenshots, and a landing page.

In this episode I walk through the full process: how I accidentally stumbled onto the idea while doing product research, how I validated it in minutes, how I reverse engineered Claude's internal API to fetch full conversation content, and my exact step-by-step workflow for shipping a Chrome extension as fast as possible.

If you use Claude daily and have ever lost a conversation because you couldn't search what was actually inside it - this extension is for you, and it's completely free.

Extension live on the Chrome Web Store: https://codedcitadel.com/apps/claude-deep-search
Subscribe to follow the $100k journey!


TIMESTAMPS
0:00 - Claude's search is broken - here's what I built to fix it
0:39 - How I look for new Chrome extension ideas (Reddit scraping method)
0:58 - Scraping the Chrome extension subreddit since October - result: nothing useful
1:06 - Finding the idea by accident while using Claude
1:17 - Validating the idea: Googling the problem and checking alternatives
1:44 - All existing alternatives are paid - so I built a free one
1:48 - First step: prototype in the browser before touching the extension
1:57 - Reverse engineering Claude's internal API via the network tab
2:16 - Fetching all conversations + full content successfully
2:30 - Setting up the samples folder as AI reference context
2:43 - Matching Claude's UI: copying HTML for the search button and modal
2:53 - Extracting CSS variables directly from Claude's website
3:01 - Using Claude to generate the Cursor prompt (token-saving trick)
3:14 - One prompt, almost working - fixing the conversation storage bug
3:27 - Adding word highlighting (and its current limitation with long chats)
3:44 - Logo design with ChatGPT - search icon + Claude colors + C letter
3:59 - Generating Chrome Web Store screenshots with ChatGPT
4:15 - Publishing to the Chrome Web Store
4:21 - The Coded Citadel landing page - live stats from all extensions
4:36 - What's coming in Episode 4

videos.title: I Reverse Engineered Youtube's API to Code This... | VibeCoding Until I Make $100k | Ep. 2
videos.description:
The only way through is forward!

In Episode 2 of my VibeCoding Until I Make $100k series, I'm building my second Chrome extension - a YouTube video filter that lets you sort search results by view count, video length, and more. But before writing a single line of code, I walk through my data-driven product research process: why I scraped Etsy sellers on Reddit, what I found (and didn't find), and how a frustrating YouTube search session gave me the idea.

I break down the full build process - reverse engineering YouTube's internal JSON API using the browser network tab, generating a native-looking YouTube UI with ChatGPT and YouTube's own CSS variables, and using Claude to build clean HTML/CSS. I also document the debugging rabbit hole that cost me hours: YouTube's pagination token system and why I was silently fetching invisible videos the whole time.

This is a no-cuts, honest look at how a solo developer ships a working Chrome extension MVP from idea to live product - bugs, dead ends, and all.

If you're interested in Chrome extension development, vibe coding, building in public, or making money with side projects in 2025, this series is for you.

Subscribe for weekly build logs.

--

TIMESTAMPS
0:00 - Welcome back + what we built in Episode 1 (YouTube Comments Scraper)
0:49 - Why I use data instead of creativity alone for product ideas
1:01 - Scraping Etsy sellers on Reddit looking for pain points
1:26 - Why Etsy sellers are a great monetization target
1:44 - Dead end: Etsy ideas didn't pan out
1:58 - Pivoting to YouTube search - finding the real idea
2:16 - Deciding to build a YouTube video filter Chrome extension
2:22 - Reverse engineering YouTube's network tab to find the JSON API
2:52 - Building a fetch function to pull YouTube video data on demand
3:14 - First working prototype - fetching views, duration, and metadata
3:47 - Using ChatGPT to design the UI using YouTube's own CSS variables
4:30 - Logo design and making it transparent
5:09 - Building the final HTML/CSS with Claude
5:36 - Setting up the project structure and AI reference folder
6:08 - Testing time - and realizing it's broken
6:36 - Deep dive into how YouTube's pagination token system works
7:11 - Bug fixed - live demo: filtering by length and minimum view count
7:51 - Known limitation: "People also watched" section not yet supported
8:02 - MVP is done - what's next

videos.title: This is How I Plan to Make $100k by Coding | VibeCoding Until I Make $100k | Ep. 1
videos.description:
This is bound to be a long journey.

Episode 1 of my VibeCoding Until I Make $100k series - and we're starting at $0. I'm a software engineer with 10+ years of experience, and I'm documenting every step of building a Chrome extension portfolio from scratch using AI tools like Claude and ChatGPT.

In this first episode I lay out the full strategy: why I'm starting with Chrome extensions (low competition, no hosting costs, built-in discovery via the Chrome Web Store), why I'm launching everything free first to build traction, and how I'm using AI as my co-developer throughout the entire process.

Then I build the first extension live - a YouTube Comments Scraper that lets you bulk export comments from individual videos or entire playlists, and send them straight to Claude or ChatGPT for analysis. This tool is the backbone of my product research system for finding real user pain points before building anything.

I also walk through my full workflow: designing UIs with ChatGPT, coding HTML/CSS with Claude using BEM methodology, wiring up Supabase Edge Functions to keep API keys secure, building a smart review/feedback modal system, and packaging the extension for the Chrome Web Store.
No fluff. No funnel. Just a real build-in-public series.

Subscribe to follow the journey weekly!

App made in this episode: https://codedcitadel.com/apps/yt-comments-exporter
All apps: https://codedcitadel.com/apps/

---

TIMESTAMPS
0:00 - The challenge: vibe coding to $100k
0:40 - Why most "$X challenge" channels aren't being honest
0:53 - My background: 10 years as a software engineer
1:18 - The strategy: build free first, then monetize
2:13 - Why Chrome extensions specifically (pros breakdown)
2:57 - First extension: YouTube Comments Scraper - the why

10:27 - Publishing to the Chrome Web Store - the packaging process
